task: Add token-bucket rate-limiting middleware to an Express JSON API, config-driven from config/ratelimit.json, covered by tests, without regressing the existing endpoint contract
loop_altitude_rationale: Multi-file change (middleware + config loader + tests) on a public request path, but reversible, dev-only, and single-agent. Stages are strictly sequential (each consumes the prior stage's stable artifact), so sequential gated stages with no parallel fan-out — medium, not large.

selection_log.why: runnable checks answer done without a human reading output: `npm test api:contract` (regression), `npm test api:ratelimit` (429/200/Retry-After/per-client), and a config-load assertion — done is machine-decidable, so it is a loop.

seam A: characterize hands a GREEN captured contract/regression suite to implement (implement must keep it green); seam B: implement hands a working 429 middleware + loaded config to verify. Each handoff is a stable, checkable artifact the upstream can prove without the downstream existing, consumed unchanged, non-conflicting surfaces -> both boundaries pass the seam test -> staged with 3 stages.

characterize searches/pins the existing contract (unknown extent -> explore_narrow); implement is a risky middleware change on the request path needing mid-flight correction (plan_execute_verify); verify is a mechanical retryable re-assertion of the full contract + ratelimit suite (retry).

dev-only, reversible, and the checks discriminate well (ratelimit test asserts 200-below AND 429-above, per-client keying) so a wrong iteration is caught by a check, not silently shipped -> human reviews at the gates, not every iteration.

the three stages form a strict dependency chain (characterize -> implement -> verify); there is no pair of independent stages that could run concurrently and benefit from fan-out -> sequential single-agent, not large.

characterize cap 3 escalate (a red baseline is an environment problem, not our change); implement cap 8 loopback->characterize (a leaked regression means the baseline needs re-pinning); verify cap 5 loopback->implement; outer escalate on any needed public-API/response-schema change.

the gate checks are fast/cheap (npm test in seconds), so frequent small passes give near-free feedback and beat few long ones. Tuned accordingly: implement cap raised to 8, verify pattern is retry (cheap re-attempt), per-pass scope kept to one slice (add one ratelimit behavior at a time). characterize stays low-cap because it is a one-shot pinning, not iterative.

definition_of_done.goal: the existing public-endpoint contract suite passes on the untouched baseline and is captured (as a git tag `ratelimit-baseline`) as the regression set later stages must keep green

feedback_signal.check: npm test api:contract && git tag -f ratelimit-baseline
feedback_signal.falsifiable_when: any existing endpoint returns a different status or body than the captured baseline expects -> the contract suite exits non-zero
feedback_signal.passing_but_wrong: a stale/empty contract suite that vacuously passes with zero assertions -> ruled out: the suite asserts a concrete status+body per endpoint and CI fails the suite if it collects zero tests

failure: contract suite cannot be made green on the untouched baseline (environment broken, not our change)

definition_of_done.goal: token-bucket middleware reads threshold+window from config/ratelimit.json and returns 429 past the threshold with a Retry-After header, per client, while the captured contract regression set stays green

feedback_signal.check: npm test api:ratelimit && npm test api:contract
feedback_signal.falsifiable_when: the (threshold+1)th request in the window still returns 200 (no 429), or the config values are ignored (a changed config/ratelimit.json threshold does not change the observed cutover), or any contract test regresses -> a test exits non-zero
feedback_signal.passing_but_wrong: middleware returns 429 for EVERY request (even below the threshold), passing a naive 'a 429 appeared' test -> ruled out: the ratelimit suite asserts 200 at/below the threshold AND 429 only above it, AND drives the threshold from config so a hardcoded limit fails the config-parametrized test

failure: 3 consecutive same-type failures on the ratelimit boundary
the contract regression set goes red (a behavior change leaked into an existing endpoint)

definition_of_done.goal: the full contract suite + the full ratelimit suite pass together, and no test assertion was weakened relative to the ratelimit-baseline tag

feedback_signal.check: npm test api:contract && npm test api:ratelimit && git diff --exit-code ratelimit-baseline -- test/
feedback_signal.falsifiable_when: any contract or ratelimit test fails together, OR a test file was weakened since the baseline tag (git diff finds a change under test/) -> non-zero exit
feedback_signal.passing_but_wrong: the agent loosens a ratelimit assertion so both suites go green -> ruled out: `git diff --exit-code ratelimit-baseline -- test/` fails on ANY test change since the pinned baseline tag, and the tag was captured in stage 1 before any code landed

failure: suites cannot both pass without weakening a test
a test file diverged from the baseline tag and cannot be justified

maker_checker.scope: a fresh-context reviewer sees only the diff + the per-stage checks + config/ratelimit.json; flags any behavior change to an existing endpoint, a hardcoded limit that ignores config, or a weakened test — not general style

planner.mandate: turn the goal into the staged spec + the negotiated contract; write config/ratelimit.json's schema expectations; never write middleware or test code

generator.mandate: write the middleware + config loader + tests to satisfy the contract; may NOT weaken a test, grade its own output, or hardcode the threshold

evaluator.mandate: a fresh codex exec that never saw the impl or the generator's reasoning; given only the diff + contract.md; assume the limiter is broken and prove it — send threshold+1 requests and check for 429, edit config/ratelimit.json and confirm the cutover moves, race two clients, and diff test/ against the baseline tag for any loosened assertion

assertions.must: every pre-existing public endpoint returns its captured baseline status + body (no regression)
the (threshold+1)th request inside the window returns HTTP 429; every request at or below the threshold returns 200
the 429 response carries a Retry-After header with a positive integer seconds value
the limiter is keyed per client so one client cannot exhaust another client's quota
the threshold and window come from config/ratelimit.json — changing the config file changes the observed cutover, no hardcoded limit
a malformed config/ratelimit.json fails fast at startup with a clear error rather than silently disabling the limiter
the window resets: after the window elapses, a client is served 200 again
the full contract suite and full ratelimit suite pass together at the end
assertions.check: npm test api:contract && npm test api:ratelimit

assertions.must: no test assertion was weakened relative to the pinned baseline over the whole run

harness_primitives: git tag `ratelimit-baseline` pinned in stage 1 as the immutable regression reference
external scratch ledger (.loop/run-state.md) holding which stage is done, the last green checkpoint, and what's next across context resets
contract.md holding the negotiated assertions (the graded criteria)
AGENTS.md standing instruction: each codex exec re-reads .loop/run-state.md + contract.md before acting

stop_conditions.success: characterize, implement, and verify each pass their check in dependency order, the diff stays within the middleware + config loader + tests, and no test diverged from the ratelimit-baseline tag
failure: any stage exhausts its max_iterations without passing its check
verify forces a redesign of implement twice without converging
escalate: a public-API or response-schema change becomes necessary to meet the limit
the contract itself is found to be wrong (an assertion contradicts a real requirement)

risk_guards.risk: reward hacking / test overfitting (agent weakens a ratelimit test to pass)
risk_guards.mitigation: the verify stage diffs test/ against the pinned baseline tag; any test change since baseline fails the gate

risk_guards.risk: config ignored / hardcoded threshold passing a naive test
risk_guards.mitigation: A5 drives the threshold from config/ratelimit.json so a hardcoded limit fails the config-parametrized test

risk_guards.risk: error amplification (a wrong characterize baseline poisons later gates)
risk_guards.mitigation: stage gates run in dependency order; a leaked regression loops implement back to characterize to re-pin, rather than head-banging in place
dev-only; no production resources; evaluator runs --sandbox read-only, generator --sandbox workspace-write
